entities.name: MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop (Pennisetum glaucum) evaluated for grain yield and water use efficiency in the study.

entities.name: COWPEA
entities.description: A legume crop (Vigna unguiculata) used in the cropping systems.

entities.name: ZIZIPHUS
entities.description: A tree species (Ziziphus mauritiana) integrated into the agroforestry system to improve productivity.

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The overall output and efficiency of the farming system.

entities.name: WATER USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency with which plants use water (WUE).

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: The ability of soil to sustain plant growth, affected by nutrient availability.

entities.name: SOIL ORGANIC CARBON
entities.description: SOC, a measure of carbon content in the soil used to assess degradation.

entities.name: POTASSIUM
entities.description: K, an essential soil nutrient measured in the study.

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: N, an essential soil nutrient measured in the study.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain harvested per unit area, specifically for millet.

entities.name: ANNUAL INCOME
entities.description: The yearly financial gain for farmers resulting from the cropping systems.

entities.name: AGROFORESTRY SYSTEM
entities.description: A farming system combining Ziziphus trees with millet and cowpea crops.

entities.name: CROPPING SYSTEM
entities.description: The pattern of crops grown on a piece of land, including mono-cropping and intercropping.

entities.name: MONO-CROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing a single crop species, such as millet or cowpea, on a piece of land.

entities.name: INTERCROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing two or more crops, such as millet and cowpea, simultaneously on the same field.

entities.name: FACTORIAL DESIGN
entities.description: A 3x4 experimental design used to compare cropping systems and fertilizer treatments.

entities.name: NPK FERTILIZER
entities.description: A chemical fertilizer containing nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium.

entities.name: FARMYARD MANURE
entities.description: FYM, an organic fertilizer used to improve soil fertility and crop yield.

entities.name: DRYLANDS
entities.description: Arid or semi-arid regions characterized by low rainfall and resource-poor farming.

entities.name: HIGH TEMPERATURES
entities.description: Extreme heat conditions affecting the Sahel region and crop growth.

entities.name: SOIL EROSION
entities.description: The process of wearing away the topsoil, contributing to land degradation.

entities.name: NUTRIENT DEPLETION
entities.description: The state of soils being depleted of essential nutrients due to lack of fertilizer and residue removal.

entities.name: LOW INPUT FARMING
entities.description: Farming systems with minimal use of external organic or inorganic fertilizers.

entities.name: WEST AFRICA SAHEL
entities.description: The geographical region where the study was conducted.

entities.name: SADORE
entities.description: The location of the ICRISAT research station in Niger.

entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: The country where the research was performed.

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The long-term maintenance of soil and land resources.

entities.name: ECOSYSTEM SERVICES
entities.description: The benefits provided by the ecosystem to humans, ensured by the agroforestry system.
